Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Compline/.firstExpected: Examen conscientiæ vel Pater Noster totum secreto.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Compline/.firstActual: Examen conscientiæ vel «Pater Noster» totum secreto.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Compline/.citation: upstream/web/www/horas/Latin/Psalterium/Common/Rubricae.txt:129 — the corpus source carries `«Pater Noster»` with guillemets. Both Perl (strip) and compositor (preserve) are defensible renderings of the same source token; neither is liturgically wrong. Phase 3 §3h defers to the corpus author's formatting.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Compline/.notes: Pattern affects every Compline row across every policy and date. This single entry is representative; the full row-key fan-out is a follow-up bulk-populate task (see ADJUDICATION_LOG.md).

Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Lauds/9fe.firstActual: Ant. Allelúja, * Dóminus regnávit, decórem índuit, allelúja, allelúja.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Lauds/9fe.citation: Phase 3 plan §3h — the compositor does not emit `Psalmus N [M]` heading lines before each psalm in psalmody sections. Perl emits one per psalm at every Hour with psalmody. The fix lives in `packages/compositor/src/emit/sections.ts` on the psalmody-slot emission path and is deferred within §3h as a structural change; see ADJUDICATION_LOG.md for the pattern catalogue.
Rubrics 1960 - 1960/2024-01-01/Lauds/9fe.notes: Representative entry for the missing-psalm-heading pattern. Affects Lauds, Prime, Terce, Sext, None, Vespers, Compline on nearly every date across every policy. Bulk-populate once the fix lands and remaining rows are re-classified.
